Help Center

How can we help?

Find answers to common questions about Refract.

What is Refract?

Refract is an AST-powered code analysis tool that finds structural issues in AI-generated code and generates clean, documented pull requests.

How does the analysis work?

Refract clones or fetches your repository, parses every file into an AST, runs 15+ detectors, and surfaces prioritized issues with before/after diffs.

Is my code safe?

Yes. Analysis is read-only and runs client-side. We never modify your repository without explicit action, and all parsing happens locally.

What languages are supported?

Refract supports TypeScript and JavaScript (.ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx) via Babel's TypeScript plugin.

How do I apply changes?

Free users can copy patches manually. Pro and Team users can generate a pull request with all accepted changes applied.

Can I use Refract with monorepos?

Yes. Refract treats the repository as a flat file system for import resolution.

How long does analysis take?

Typical repositories (under 10,000 files) complete in under 5 minutes. Incremental analysis takes under 30 seconds.

AI writes fast. Refract makes it shippable.

Risk Disclosure: Refract provides code analysis tools and does not guarantee bug-free code. Always review proposed changes before merging.

Hypothetical Performance Disclosure: All examples and demonstrations shown are for illustrative purposes only. Actual results may vary based on your codebase, team size, and usage patterns.

Testimonials Disclosure: Testimonials appearing on this site may not be representative of all customers and do not guarantee future performance or results.
